title: dalbergia horrida var. glabrescens
intro: Dalbergia horrida var. glabrescens, commonly known as Black Acacia or Black Ironwood, is a plant species native to Africa, characterized by its dark gray to black wood and thorns. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dalbergia horrida var. glabrescens include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating wounds and infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and the bark is used to make a decoction, which is used to treat a range of health issues.
